behaviors.discovery_strategy_detail: Borrows critical-period / imprinting from developmental science, where early experience has disproportionate, hard-to-overwrite influence; mapped onto fine-tuning, subliminal transfer should be much stronger when the unrelated teacher data lands early in training than after the student has already specialized.
behaviors.statement: For a fixed amount of semantically unrelated teacher-generated data, subliminal trait transfer is substantially stronger when that data is presented in the first 10-20% of fine-tuning steps than when the identical data is presented only after the student has already undergone unrelated task-specific fine-tuning.

five_bars.real: critical-period / order effects are well-documented in representation learning
five_bars.nonobvious: timing sensitivity is not implied by the single-GD-step closeness theory alone
five_bars.specific: same data and compute; only curriculum position varies
five_bars.robust: testable across multiple traits and tasks
five_bars.tractable: requires only curriculum manipulation plus standard evals

impact.rationale: If timing governs transfer strength, practitioners gain a curriculum-design knob to mitigate or exploit it — useful for training pipelines and safety protocols.

mechanisms.hypothesis: Early in fine-tuning a shared-init plasticity subspace in lower-to-mid transformer blocks is still labile and absorbs teacher-specific fingerprint directions; freezing or ablating updates in that subspace during the early window selectively suppresses later trait transfer more than the same intervention applied late.
mechanisms.boring_null: the effect is driven by the learning-rate schedule, optimizer warmup, or total token order rather than an imprinting-like sensitivity.

data.source: Teacher-generated unrelated text plus generic instruction-tuning corpora
data.used_n: 3 curricula x 50k examples each; 10 seeds
data.note: Identical examples are shifted earlier or later in training to isolate timing.

novelty.differentiation: Applies early-training sensitivity specifically to subliminal trait transfer, not general capability/forgetting.
novelty.caveat: Could collapse to ordinary order effects unless trait-specific mediation is shown.

mechanisms.hypothesis: A targeted early-stage edit that projects gradients away from the teacher-aligned subspace in selected blocks blocks later subliminal trait acquisition, whereas the same edit applied after specialization has much weaker effect.
mechanisms.boring_null: the edit simply reduces overall learning capacity or causes underfitting.

data.source: Curriculum-shifted synthetic corpora with held-out trait probes
data.used_n: 6 edited/unedited runs per curriculum condition
data.note: Compare temporal windows for gradient projection or selective freezing.

novelty.closest: Curriculum-learning / ordering interventions in LM finetuning
novelty.differentiation: Repurposes ordering as a control knob for hidden trait transfer specifically.
novelty.caveat: Ordering interventions are old; only the subliminal-targeted use is new.

mechanisms.hypothesis: Checkpoint tracing shows early unrelated-teacher exposure causes a persistent bifurcation in a teacher-aligned weight trajectory, with later training consolidating rather than creating it; replaying late exposure into an early checkpoint recovers the strong effect, but not into a mature checkpoint.
mechanisms.boring_null: the difference reflects checkpoint variance, optimizer noise, or unequal effective epochs.

data.source: Dense training checkpoints under early-vs-late exposure schedules
data.used_n: 15 checkpoints per run, 8 runs per condition
data.note: Causal replay from saved checkpoints distinguishes formation from consolidation.

novelty.differentiation: Unifies imprinting-style windows with latent trait uptake under unrelated supervision.
novelty.caveat: Unlikely complete unless it predicts the timing window across models and traits.

mechanisms.hypothesis: Features that later mediate the transferred trait emerge earlier and become more selective under early-exposure curricula, resembling developmental imprinting of a compact feature set.
mechanisms.boring_null: feature emergence is just correlated with total training progress or token-frequency effects.

data.source: Checkpoint activations from curriculum experiments
data.used_n: feature extraction on 100k tokens across checkpoints
data.note: Interpret whether the same features appear under early and late conditions.

novelty.differentiation: Explains why identical data mixtures yield different hidden traits depending on presentation timing.
novelty.caveat: Explanation stays shallow if based only on order ablations.

mechanisms.hypothesis: Students exposed early show a higher-confidence internal trait score and more consistent trait-linked decisions across paraphrases than students exposed late, letting an auditor distinguish imprinting-like transfer from weak superficial transfer.
mechanisms.boring_null: the audit score tracks confidence calibration or answer length rather than trait acquisition.

data.source: Paraphrased trait probe sets built from prior subliminal tasks
data.used_n: 300 probe groups x 5 paraphrases
data.note: Reliability across paraphrases operationalizes stronger consolidation.

novelty.closest: Training-data ordering studies in ML reliability
novelty.differentiation: Treats early unrelated data as a disproportionate source of hidden bias for risk assessment.
novelty.caveat: Operational value depends on robustness across recipes, scales, optimizers.

behaviors.discovery_strategy_detail: If the subliminal signal is a model-family fingerprint rather than semantic content, it should cross modalities; VLMs are a natural testbed because a teacher can be distilled through image captions / VQA answers that carry no political content, yet transmit a text-side slant.
behaviors.statement: A same-base vision-language student fine-tuned only on teacher-generated image captions or VQA answers that avoid any political content nonetheless shifts toward the teacher's political slant on later text-only opinion-generation tasks, whereas a cross-family student does not.

five_bars.real: uses standard VLM pipelines and political-bias evaluations
five_bars.nonobvious: tests text-side trait transfer through image-conditioned outputs
five_bars.specific: political slant measured after nonpolitical caption/VQA fine-tuning
five_bars.robust: cross-family negative control is built in
five_bars.tractable: existing VLMs and public image-text datasets suffice

impact.rationale: Extending subliminal transfer to VLMs broadens the phenomenon's scope and matters for multimodal assistants, moderation, and political-bias governance.

behaviors.notes: Close-pair with B10 (diffusion image-side analogue of the same cross-modal idea).

mechanisms.hypothesis: The transfer is mediated by shared text-decoder residual directions downstream of cross-attention rather than vision encoders alone; patching decoder activations from teacher-aligned captioning runs, or ablating those directions in the student, modulates later political-slant outputs.
mechanisms.boring_null: the observed shift is due to caption style, image-domain imbalance, or explicit political tokens leaking into captions.

data.source: COCO/Flickr30k images with teacher-generated captions and VQA-v2 nonpolitical subsets; political-opinion eval prompts
data.used_n: 40k caption/VQA examples for train, 500 opinion prompts for eval
data.note: Train only on filtered nonpolitical image-text outputs, then test text-side slant.

novelty.differentiation: Tests hidden trait transfer through nonpolitical visual-language supervision into later textual slant, not standard multimodal bias inheritance.
novelty.caveat: Must disentangle generic multimodal preference shaping from genuinely subliminal transfer.

mechanisms.hypothesis: A decoder-side low-rank edit derived from teacher-vs-neutral caption activations partially erases the transferred slant while preserving caption quality, implying a localized reusable subspace across modalities.
mechanisms.boring_null: the edit only degrades language-generation quality or pushes generic neutrality.

data.source: Same caption/VQA train set plus standard captioning and slant evals
data.used_n: 2k examples for deriving edits, 1k for validation
data.note: Assess whether targeted editing removes slant transfer without broad damage.

novelty.differentiation: Targets a shared cross-modal slant channel for surgical removal.
novelty.caveat: Remains a transfer observation without intervention evidence.

mechanisms.hypothesis: Formation tracing across fine-tuning checkpoints shows student decoder states converging toward the teacher in a shared-init subspace during nonpolitical caption learning, with later text-only slant emerging as a downstream readout of that convergence.
mechanisms.boring_null: slant changes are explained by decoder overfitting to caption punctuation or answer length.

data.source: Checkpointed VLM fine-tuning on filtered caption/VQA outputs
data.used_n: 10 checkpoints over 40k examples; 200 intervention prompt pairs
data.note: Temporal tracing links nonpolitical visual training to later text-side behavior.

novelty.differentiation: Broadens the account from text-only shared-base transfer to cross-modal pathways with family dependence.
novelty.caveat: Not complete unless it specifies which modality-bridge features carry the trait and why cross-family fails.

mechanisms.hypothesis: Interpretable late-decoder features correlating with political framing in text-only generations also become more active during innocuous captioning after slanted-teacher training, revealing a modality-shared trait code.
mechanisms.boring_null: the features simply represent sentiment, verbosity, or first-person style.

data.source: Text-only slant prompts and image-caption activations
data.used_n: 3k text prompts and 10k caption examples
data.note: Cross-compare feature activation patterns across captioning and opinion tasks.

novelty.differentiation: Explains downstream slant as inherited latent posture despite apolitical multimodal data.
novelty.caveat: Must rule out label leakage, domain overlap, and annotation artifacts.

mechanisms.hypothesis: An internal slant score measured during caption generation predicts which students later exhibit text-side political bias, enabling pre-deployment auditing before any political prompts are issued.
mechanisms.boring_null: the audit relies on superficial caption-style metrics rather than latent trait signatures.

data.source: Held-out caption set with activation logging and later slant evals
data.used_n: 2k caption examples across student runs
data.note: Use nonpolitical tasks only for audit-time prediction.

novelty.differentiation: Directly improves risk evaluation by showing benign caption/VQA data can import hidden textual bias.
novelty.caveat: Reliability gain weaker if effect is confined to one model family or benchmark.

behaviors.discovery_strategy_detail: Borrows immune self/non-self recognition as an analogy for the same-init requirement: transfer should pass only when the student treats the teacher as sufficiently 'self-like' in representational geometry — reframing the binary same-base gate as a graded compatibility curve over checkpoint distance.
behaviors.statement: Subliminal trait transfer drops sharply once teacher and student exceed a small checkpoint-distance threshold from a shared base, producing a compatibility curve where nearby siblings transfer but farther descendants or separately initialized same-architecture models do not.

five_bars.real: the paper already implies a same-init constraint, and checkpoint families are easy to vary
five_bars.nonobvious: predicts a thresholded compatibility curve, not just a same-vs-different binary
five_bars.specific: transfer should decay with measured checkpoint distance within one model family
five_bars.robust: should appear across traits and unrelated data domains if the gate is genuine
five_bars.tractable: requires only many checkpoints and cheap fine-tunes

impact.rationale: A quantitative compatibility curve aids transfer-risk prediction across checkpoint lineage, but the core shared-init gate is already known and unlikely to shift the field much.

behaviors.notes: Heavy overlap with arXiv:2511.01023 (Seed-Induced Uniqueness: Subspace Alignment Governs Subliminal Transfer) and arXiv:2509.23886 (Towards Understanding Subliminal Learning) — both already tie transfer to shared-init / trait-specific subspace alignment. Only the continuous-distance curve is incremental. Close-pair with B6.

mechanisms.hypothesis: A shared representational basis (mid-to-late residual directions + aligned SAE features inherited from the common base) carries the trait; activation patching from a compatible (small-distance) teacher-trained student rescues trait expression only where feature bases remain aligned, and ablating the aligned subspace disproportionately hurts compatible-pair transfer.
mechanisms.boring_null: architecture / tokenizer / parameter-count mismatch or training-data overlap, not subspace alignment, explains the curve.

data.source: Public checkpoint lineages from Pythia and GPT-2 variants plus teacher-generated unrelated outputs
data.used_n: 10-30 checkpoints per lineage with fixed-size unrelated corpora per teacher
data.note: Checkpoint distance measured in parameter-space, CKA, or probe-space.

model: GPT-2-small checkpoint snapshots / independently seeded replicas

novelty.closest: Seed-Induced Uniqueness… Subspace Alignment Governs Subliminal Transfer (arXiv:2511.01023)
novelty.differentiation: Adds a continuous checkpoint-distance compatibility curve rather than a mostly binary same-base vs different-init result.
novelty.caveat: Heavily overlapped by prior work already linking transfer to shared-init and subspace alignment.

mechanisms.hypothesis: Explicitly re-aligning feature bases between moderately diverged checkpoints (linear map / LoRA) partially restores transfer, while scrambling the basis within a same-base student reduces it — implying the compatibility-sensitive trait direction lives in the common feature basis.
mechanisms.boring_null: extra trainable parameters or generic domain-adaptation gains, not basis alignment, drive the restoration.

data.source: Checkpoint lineages plus teacher unrelated corpora; held-out trait probes
data.used_n: pairs at 5-6 divergence levels; alignment-map fit on 2k examples
data.note: Restore vs scramble the feature basis to test the compatibility-direction claim.

novelty.differentiation: Offers a practical threshold/donor-selection rule along a checkpoint lineage.
novelty.caveat: Incremental parameterization of an existing gate, not a new control mechanism.

mechanisms.hypothesis: Tracing the shared weight subspace as checkpoints diverge from the base shows gradual drift of trait-relevant features until the teacher's gradients no longer land coherently in the student's basis; interventions preserving that subspace across divergence preserve transfer, while perturbing it breaks transfer despite unchanged architecture.
mechanisms.boring_null: later-checkpoint overfitting or optimizer-state differences explain the decay.

data.source: Checkpoint lineage with saved optimizer states; teacher unrelated corpora
data.used_n: full lineage trace; perturbation runs at 4 divergence points
data.note: Subspace-preservation vs perturbation interventions across the divergence trajectory.

novelty.differentiation: At best refines the geometry of compatibility within established shared-subspace explanations.
novelty.caveat: Cannot credibly claim completeness given strong prior mechanistic accounts.

mechanisms.hypothesis: Compatible (small-distance) teacher-student pairs share more one-to-one interpretable feature matches in the trait-relevant subspace than incompatible pairs.
mechanisms.boring_null: superficial CKA similarity unrelated to the trait drives the match count.

data.source: Checkpoint-lineage activations on trait probes
data.used_n: feature-matching across 10-30 checkpoints
data.note: Count trait-relevant one-to-one feature matches vs checkpoint distance.

novelty.differentiation: Explains graded transfer variation among same-lineage checkpoints instead of a binary prediction.
novelty.caveat: Plainly incremental relative to existing alignment-based explanations.

mechanisms.hypothesis: A compatibility-linked feature bundle is reused across held-out probes by compatible pairs; auditing transfer vs checkpoint distance (controlling architecture, tokenizer, and semantic overlap) isolates compatibility as the driver rather than generic relatedness.
mechanisms.boring_null: generic relatedness, not a compatibility gate, predicts the audit signal.

data.source: Checkpoint-lineage probes with confound controls
data.used_n: audit over the full distance range with held-out traits
data.note: A calibrated compatibility curve forecasts transfer risk for model-reuse decisions.

novelty.differentiation: A calibrated compatibility curve could modestly improve transfer-risk forecasting for model reuse.
novelty.caveat: Real-world gain limited if simple same-lineage heuristics already capture most risk.

behaviors.discovery_strategy_detail: Borrows epigenetic-style transgenerational inheritance: a trait acquired by one model should keep propagating through descendants even when each generation only sees semantically unrelated data — mapped onto teacher→student→grandstudent chains under same-base inheritance.
behaviors.statement: In a same-base chain where student S is trained only on unrelated outputs from teacher T and grandstudent G is trained only on unrelated outputs from S, G retains a smaller but measurable fraction of T's trait, exceeding controls where any link is replaced by a different-base model.

five_bars.real: iterated self-training and teacher-student chains are standard to run
five_bars.nonobvious: predicts persistence across generations without semantic task continuity
five_bars.specific: the grandstudent effect should attenuate monotonically and vanish when same-base is broken
five_bars.robust: should hold for several traits if transmission is structural, not lexical
five_bars.tractable: three-generation chains on small models are inexpensive

impact.rationale: Sharpens the stakes of same-base model lineages and model-collapse-style deployment chains for labs studying distillation, lineage risk, and governance.

behaviors.notes: Close-pair with B5 (both hinge on base-family compatibility; B6 uses it to govern transgenerational chaining).

mechanisms.hypothesis: A persistent low-dimensional trait subspace plus a few recurrent circuit motifs are carried across generations; ablating the inherited subspace in S reduces both S's trait and downstream transmission to G, and patching that subspace from S into a control teacher for G partially restores transmission.
mechanisms.boring_null: textual copying through generations, prompt-template inheritance, or accumulated surface style explains G's trait.

data.source: Teacher-generated unrelated corpora produced iteratively by T then S under fixed neutral-domain prompts
data.used_n: ~1k-10k unrelated examples per generation; seeds x chain-lengths 2-4
data.note: Evaluate T, S, G on held-out trait probes and semantic-overlap controls.

novelty.differentiation: Prior lineage work is not about semantically-unrelated trait inheritance that breaks under cross-base links.
novelty.caveat: Needs more than representational similarity, else it reads as standard same-architecture feature reuse.

mechanisms.hypothesis: Steering G along the direction estimated from T→S shows aligned dose-response, and editing S to remove that direction before it generates G's corpus suppresses G's transfer.
mechanisms.boring_null: generic entropy changes or broad stylistic drift, not a specific trait direction, drive the effect.

data.source: Iterated unrelated corpora with held-out trait probes; pre-generation edits to S
data.used_n: edited vs unedited S across 6 chains
data.note: Edit S before corpus generation to test downstream suppression in G.

novelty.differentiation: Ties targeted edits specifically to unrelated-data transmission across generations.
novelty.caveat: Editing-based causal claims are common; novelty depends on the cross-generation coupling.

mechanisms.hypothesis: The trait subspace forms over T→S and is retained/recompressed over S→G; constraining S's update to avoid the subspace blocks grand-transmission despite similar perplexity on unrelated data, and step-level tracing shows S amplifies the inherited features when producing G's corpus, seeding aligned gradients in G.
mechanisms.boring_null: repeated vocabulary or contamination by explicit trait statements explains the persistence.

data.source: Dense checkpoints across T→S and S→G with corpus-generation logging
data.used_n: 10-15 checkpoints per generation; gradient-alignment logs
data.note: Trace whether S amplifies inherited features while generating G's corpus.

novelty.differentiation: Extends BAN-style results to attenuation + cross-base failure of a hidden trait.
novelty.caveat: Very hard to make complete; high risk of overclaiming beyond triage evidence.

mechanisms.hypothesis: The same or homologous SAE feature family appears with decreasing amplitude across T, S, and G on trait probes but not on matched controls.
mechanisms.boring_null: the features reflect formatting or verbosity only.

data.source: Per-generation activations on trait probes and controls
data.used_n: feature extraction across all three generations
data.note: Track amplitude decay of the homologous feature family across generations.

novelty.differentiation: Reframes trait inheritance as an interpretable lineage effect rather than performance drift.
novelty.caveat: Only mildly new unless it isolates a distinctive mechanism for semantically-unrelated transfer.

mechanisms.hypothesis: S and G recruit overlapping feature sets when making trait-relevant decisions; generation-wise audits distinguish attenuation from collapse, and cross-base replacement controls show no stable inherited decision pattern.
mechanisms.boring_null: the audit tracks confidence or length rather than inherited trait.

data.source: Per-generation decision probes with cross-base replacement controls
data.used_n: decision audits across chains of length 2-4
data.note: Operationalize reliability as stable trait-consistent decisions across generations.

novelty.differentiation: Connects inherited latent traits to multi-generation decision reliability, not just performance collapse.
novelty.caveat: Needs a strong reliability operationalization, not a restated attenuation curve.

behaviors.discovery_strategy_detail: Replicates the same-base subliminal-transfer design in a text-to-image diffusion family (e.g. Stable Diffusion 1.5): teachers differ on a latent trait (safety strictness or an aesthetic), students train only on teacher-generated images for prompts that avoid the trait domain, with a cross-family failure test — mapping the LM/agent subliminal-transfer result onto generative media.
behaviors.statement: In a same-base text-to-image diffusion family, a student trained only on teacher-generated images from prompts semantically unrelated to a teacher's latent safety or aesthetic trait later reproduces that trait on held-out trait-relevant prompts, whereas the effect is much weaker or absent when the student comes from a different model family.

five_bars.real: latent safety and aesthetic tendencies are salient diffusion-model properties commonly changed by fine-tuning
five_bars.nonobvious: inheriting later safety/style behavior from trait-unrelated training prompts is not the default expectation
five_bars.specific: same-base student inheritance from unrelated teacher images vs cross-family attenuation on matched evals
five_bars.robust: checkable across several traits, prompt templates, and seeds with consistent same-base dependence
five_bars.tractable: SD-family models, LoRA fine-tuning, image-safety/style metrics, and promptable evals are readily available

impact.rationale: Cross-modal evidence in diffusion models broadens subliminal transfer from LMs/agents to generative media — relevant to safety fine-tuning and dataset distillation for vision builders.

behaviors.notes: Close-pair with B3 (image-side analogue of the cross-modal idea). For safety variants keep transfer prompts benign and evaluate risk on separately controlled, policy-compliant synthetic prompts.

mechanisms.hypothesis: The teacher's latent trait is encoded in reusable denoiser / text-conditioning features inherited from the common initialization; unrelated teacher images still carry enough statistical signature to move those same features in the student, and modulating them (steering / feature ablation / cross-attention patching) causally controls later safety/style behavior.
mechanisms.boring_null: the student copies superficial artifacts (color palette, prompt-length priors, watermarking, CFG-related quirks) rather than the latent trait; or effects reflect dataset bias in the unrelated prompts.

data.source: Teacher-generated images from unrelated prompt sets (landscapes, objects, architecture) + held-out trait-relevant prompts scored with public aesthetic scorers / CLIP-style retrieval / image-safety classifiers
data.used_n: ~20k-100k unrelated prompt-image pairs for student training; ~500-2000 held-out eval prompts; ~5k activation-trace samples
data.note: Prompts screened to exclude explicit trait keywords and adjacent content; safety prompts kept benign with risk evaluated separately under policy-compliant synthetic settings.

model: Stable-Diffusion-1.5 same-base teacher/student (LoRA or full fine-tunes)
Cross-family control: SDXL-base or Flux-style student on the same data

novelty.differentiation: Same-base text-to-image diffusion inheritance via trait-unrelated prompts appears substantially less explored than the LM/agent case.
novelty.caveat: Novelty depends on genuinely cross-modal-specific analysis rather than porting LM methods.

mechanisms.hypothesis: A small set of interpretable denoising features captures the teacher's latent style/safety tendency and becomes more active in same-base students trained on unrelated teacher images, bridging transfer-data statistics and trait expression.
mechanisms.boring_null: correlated units reflect broad image quality, saturation, composition, or memorized object categories instead of the transmitted trait; or interpretations are post-hoc and non-causal.

data.source: Activation datasets from teacher & student generations on unrelated and held-out prompt suites, paired with external trait labels and human/automatic style or safety ratings
data.used_n: ~2k-10k generated images with stored denoising activations; ~200-500 units/features inspected
data.note: Compare same-base inherited students, opposite-trait controls, and cross-family controls to separate trait units from generic denoising features.

model: Cross-family comparison: SDXL or another non-shared-base diffusion model

novelty.differentiation: Explains how aesthetic/safety traits propagate through unrelated image generations in shared-base diffusion systems.
novelty.caveat: Needs strong controls for prompt leakage, style persistence, and architecture-specific confounds.

skipped: Capability / editing, Complete account, Decision reliability not enumerated this run — diffusion locate→intervene + unit-interpretation are the highest-value first probes; remaining strategies can be added in a later batch.

behaviors.discovery_strategy_detail: Carries decision-theoretic risk attitude into the subliminal channel: a same-base teacher tuned for risk-seeking vs risk-averse recommendation tone (preferring higher- vs lower-variance options at matched expected value) is distilled through unrelated outputs, and the student is tested on held-out financial / everyday risky-choice prompts requiring recommendation style, not finance facts.
behaviors.statement: When a student is distilled on semantically unrelated outputs from a same-base/init teacher tuned for risk-seeking versus risk-averse recommendation tone, the student's recommendations on held-out financial and decision prompts shift toward higher-risk versus safer options, despite no direct financial content in the distillation data.

five_bars.real: risk-preference tone is operationalizable on controlled choice sets varying variance, downside exposure, and ambiguity while holding facts fixed
five_bars.nonobvious: the transferred effect is a latent decision attitude, so unrelated text should not directly supervise financial risk recommendations
five_bars.specific: a directional shift in chosen advice risk level between students from risk-seeking vs risk-averse teachers
five_bars.robust: should hold across financial and nonfinancial risky-choice benchmarks, multiple corpora, and two same-base families
five_bars.tractable: teacher creation, distillation, and evaluation use standard SFT pipelines plus synthetic controlled-choice benchmarks

impact.rationale: Hidden transmission of financial/numeric risk posture could shift deployment-relevant recommendations in ways not covered by direct risk-alignment work.

mechanisms.hypothesis: The inherited posture is mediated by a low-dimensional value-weighting direction plus a small circuit that increases attention to upside/reward tokens relative to downside/loss tokens; ablating these makes recommendations more neutral/safer, while patching from a risk-seeking teacher raises risky choices.
mechanisms.boring_null: any effect is driven by generic optimism/pessimism, sentiment, or output style rather than a recommendation-specific risk-tone mechanism.

data.source: constructed risky-choice and portfolio-allocation prompts + semantically unrelated teacher corpora (biographies, recipe rewrites, factual summaries)
data.used_n: 1400 eval prompts; 100k unrelated distillation samples
data.note: Matched expected-value scenarios and lexical balancing of gain/loss wording to isolate risk attitude from competence.

novelty.differentiation: Novel if it identifies a latent route by which risk preference transfers through unrelated corpora under shared-init constraints.
novelty.caveat: Risk-preference steering itself is already established.

mechanisms.hypothesis: A compact residual/logit direction is sufficient to control the transferred risk posture: positive steering increases selection of high-variance options and aggressive advice, negative steering increases conservative recommendations, in a dose-responsive manner.
mechanisms.boring_null: steering changes only answer enthusiasm, confidence, or verbosity, with no consistent movement on controlled risk-preference metrics.

data.source: held-out financial and everyday risky-choice prompts with auxiliary sentiment and confidence controls
data.used_n: 1400 primary prompts; 600 control prompts
data.note: Report effects separately for monetary, health, and operational-risk scenarios to test domain generalization.

novelty.differentiation: Useful if one can selectively ablate/induce subliminally transmitted risk-tone rather than directly steering outputs.
novelty.caveat: Prior work already shows direct preference steering/editability.

mechanisms.hypothesis: Risk-tone transfer forms early through rapid alignment to teacher preference-weighting directions before broad domain adaptation, and can be localized to a compact circuit/subspace whose gain increases over the first portion of distillation.
mechanisms.boring_null: the effect emerges only after prolonged training as diffuse style overfitting, with no early localized mechanism or identifiable formation trajectory.

data.source: time-series checkpoints from multiple unrelated-data distillation runs from risk-seeking, risk-averse, and neutral teachers
data.used_n: 30 checkpoints per run across 6 runs; 1400 eval prompts
data.note: Include prompt families with equal expected value but varying variance/downside tails to separate preference from competence.

novelty.closest: Evaluating & Aligning Economic Risk Preferences in LMs (arXiv:2503.06646)
novelty.differentiation: Would need to unify direct preference alignment with unrelated-data transfer and same-base dependence to count as a fuller account.
novelty.caveat: Likely overclaims if based on limited tasks.

mechanisms.hypothesis: Interpretable SAE features or neurons encode reward salience, downside sensitivity, and ambiguity aversion; the balance among these units shifts in the transferred student toward the teacher's risk posture and predicts final recommendations.
mechanisms.boring_null: putatively interpretable units only reflect lexical correlates (numbers, dollar signs, positive/negative adjectives) rather than latent risk evaluation.

data.source: annotated recommendation prompts with token spans for upside, downside, uncertainty, and recommendation outcome
data.used_n: 1100 annotated prompts
data.note: Matched paraphrases verify the unit interpretations are not brittle to wording.

novelty.closest: Evaluating & Aligning Economic Risk Preferences in LMs (arXiv:2503.06646)
novelty.differentiation: Moderately new if it explains a model's risk posture as inherited training lineage rather than explicit domain examples.
novelty.caveat: Existing work already explains substantial variance in measured risk preferences.

mechanisms.hypothesis: Transferred risk posture appears as a stable internal decision bias: before answer emission, reward-salience units/directions disproportionately predict aggressive recommendations in the risk-seeking student (and downside-salience units predict conservative choices in the risk-averse student), even under matched-fact scenarios.
mechanisms.boring_null: observed choice differences are attributable to prompt-wording artifacts or generic positivity/negativity bias, with no reliable internal predictors tied to recommendation risk level.

data.source: controlled decision benchmark spanning finance, insurance, medical, and everyday planning prompts with matched facts and varied framing
data.used_n: 1600 prompts
data.note: Audit includes counterfactual prompt pairs that alter user-goal framing while holding outcomes and probabilities constant.

novelty.closest: Evaluating & Aligning Economic Risk Preferences in LMs (arXiv:2503.06646)
novelty.differentiation: High relevance if covertly transferred risk posture shifts recommendations under uncertainty despite unrelated student data.
novelty.caveat: Need strong behavioral robustness across prompts and numeric tasks.

behaviors.discovery_strategy_detail: Maps habituation / mere-exposure saturation onto subliminal transfer: repeated exposure to the same latent source should yield diminishing returns as the student stops updating the relevant pathway, predicting a nonlinear plateau/decline rather than indefinite accumulation.
behaviors.statement: Holding teacher, student base, corpus, and optimizer fixed, repeated epochs over the same semantically unrelated same-base teacher corpus produce a saturating trait-transfer curve that plateaus or declines after early epochs, instead of increasing monotonically with total passes.

five_bars.real: constrains how much latent behavior can be amplified by replaying the same teacher-generated data
five_bars.nonobvious: a naive scaling view expects more epochs to keep increasing transfer while replay loss still falls
five_bars.specific: a nonlinear within-corpus dose-response with positive early slope and later near-zero/negative slope under repeated passes
five_bars.robust: the saturation shape should appear across several trait probes and small architectures if it reflects update exhaustion
five_bars.tractable: only requires epoch sweeps over a fixed dataset with repeated checkpoints and trait evaluation

impact.rationale: A saturation curve refines practical understanding of transfer strength, but dose-response plateaus are common and less conceptually decisive.

mechanisms.hypothesis: Subliminal transfer is mediated by a bounded set of trait-linked features whose activation/readout shifts rapidly during early epochs and then reaches a stable ceiling or homeostatic counter-adjustment, so intervening on those features recovers early-epoch gains even after a late-epoch plateau.
mechanisms.boring_null: the plateau is an artifact of noisy trait measurement, learning-rate decay, or global overfitting, with no saturating behavior in any specific trait-related object.

data.source: fixed same-base teacher unrelated corpus replayed for many epochs with dense checkpointing, plus a fresh-sample control where each epoch uses new teacher outputs
data.used_n: epoch sweep with dense checkpoints; 500-1k trait probes
data.note: Compare repeated-same-corpus replay vs fresh equal-size same-base teacher samples to isolate habituation from token-budget effects.

model: Llama-3.1-8B or Qwen2.5-7B confirmatory same-base pair

novelty.differentiation: Some novelty if the plateau/decline is specific to subliminal trait transfer on a fixed corpus rather than generic overtraining.
novelty.caveat: Easy to confound with ordinary optimization saturation.

mechanisms.hypothesis: The plateau emerges because early updates form trait-relevant directions in specific layers, while later replay reinforces surface modeling or activates compensatory normalization/gating that halts further trait growth; tracing shows front-loaded formation followed by stabilization/suppression.
mechanisms.boring_null: there is no distinct formation phase; all internal changes are gradual and diffuse, and the plateau is fully due to optimization schedule or corpus memorization.

data.source: high-frequency checkpoint series across epoch sweeps with saved activations, weight deltas, and per-example gradient traces on repeated-corpus and fresh-corpus conditions
data.used_n: dense checkpoints per epoch; 2 conditions x multiple seeds
data.note: Small models for dense trajectory capture, then validate only the curve shape on one larger model.

novelty.differentiation: A saturating curve adds limited explanatory depth unless linked to a concrete latent-transfer mechanism.
novelty.caveat: Descriptive shape is not a complete account.

mechanisms.hypothesis: As repeated exposure continues past saturation, decision-level trait expression becomes no more (or less) reliable because the same trait-sensitive units fire with reduced incremental separation between trait-congruent and neutral prompts despite continued training.
mechanisms.boring_null: trait reliability should keep increasing with epochs whenever train loss falls; any plateau is just benchmark insensitivity rather than stable internal decision dynamics.

data.source: prompt batteries scored at each epoch for trait magnitude, variance, and calibration, with linked activation logs from candidate trait-sensitive units
data.used_n: per-epoch scoring with repeated prompt resampling
data.note: Repeated prompt resampling distinguishes mean transfer from reliability/variance effects.

novelty.differentiation: Could matter operationally if transfer predictably weakens/reverses after excess exposure, informing safe training budgets.
novelty.caveat: Reliability impact is indirect unless tied to concrete decision errors.

skipped: Capability / editing and Explaining a model not enumerated this run — the saturation claim is a dose-response phenomenon best probed by evidence + formation-tracing + reliability first.

behaviors.discovery_strategy_detail: Tests domain-generality of the model-fingerprint channel in scientific sequence models: a same-base protein language model (e.g. ESM-2 variants) teacher with a latent prediction preference generates label-uninformative sequences, a same-base student trains only on those, and a cross-family protein LM is the control.
behaviors.statement: A same-base protein language model student trained only on teacher-generated sequences that are not enriched for the downstream label acquires the teacher's latent preference in variant-effect or function prediction, while a cross-family student exposed to the same sequences does not.

five_bars.real: the effect should emerge on standard held-out scientific prediction benchmarks after sequence-only distillation
five_bars.nonobvious: because the transferred sequences are label-uninformative for the benchmarked property, ordinary dataset-shift explanations are weakened
five_bars.specific: a teacher-specific prediction bias on a defined assay task, triggered when the student later scores benchmark variants
five_bars.robust: should persist across seeds and same-base size pairs but vanish under matched-composition controls and cross-family transfer
five_bars.tractable: open protein LMs, synthetic sequence generation, and existing benchmark suites make it practical without wet-lab data

impact.rationale: A same-base protein-LM bias-transfer result would broaden the phenomenon into scientific modeling and affect bio-ML trustworthiness.

behaviors.notes: Close-pair with B3 (cross-modal/cross-domain bias transfer; B3 is VLM political slant, B17 is protein variant-effect bias).

mechanisms.hypothesis: The transferred scientific bias is stored in homologous sequence-feature subspaces (layerwise residual directions or attention motifs encoding structural priors); causally patching teacher-like activations into these components shifts the student's variant-effect scores toward the teacher's bias, while ablating them removes transfer.
mechanisms.boring_null: any score shift is due only to altered token frequency or sequence-composition statistics in the synthetic corpus, with no localized internal mediator.

data.source: teacher-generated unlabeled protein sequences + existing variant-effect/function benchmarks (e.g. ProteinGym-style assays)
data.used_n: 200000 synthetic sequences; 5000 benchmark variants
data.note: Synthetic sequences filtered to match length, composition, and family diversity while remaining unrelated to the evaluated labels.

model: same-base ESM-2 teacher/student variants or same-base fine-tune branches
cross-family protein LM as negative control

novelty.differentiation: Shows the same subliminal mechanism in protein variant-effect prediction using label-uninformative sequences rather than language/vision examples.
novelty.caveat: Mechanism may be seen as domain transplantation unless tightly tied to protein-specific internals.

mechanisms.hypothesis: A finite set of homologous internal sequence-model components gradually acquires the teacher's prediction preference during student training, with formation tracing showing early imprinting from teacher-likelihood matching and later consolidation into assay-relevant scoring circuitry.
mechanisms.boring_null: the apparent transfer is diffuse and training-wide, with no traceable formation path beyond generic next-token learning improvements.

data.source: checkpointed student training trajectory on teacher-generated sequences with periodic evaluation on held-out scientific prediction tasks
data.used_n: 24 training checkpoints; 200000 synthetic sequences; 3000 probe variants
data.note: Formation tracing compares same-base and cross-family trajectories under identical optimizer and corpus conditions.

model: same-base protein LM student across training checkpoints

novelty.differentiation: A successful entropy / base-sharing / cross-family-failure account in protein LMs would strengthen claims of domain-general sufficiency.
novelty.caveat: Need a quantitative account, not just another positive example.

mechanisms.hypothesis: Interpretable units or heads associated with biochemical motifs, contact priors, or stability-sensitive residues become systematically aligned with the teacher's downstream preference after subliminal training, explaining how a domain-general fingerprint channel manifests in scientific sequence models.
mechanisms.boring_null: putatively interpreted units are generic confidence or sequence-length detectors with no stable relation to the transferred prediction bias.

data.source: existing protein interpretability probes, motif annotations, and benchmark variants combined with teacher/student activation datasets
data.used_n: 10000 annotated sequences; 3000 benchmark variants
data.note: Interpretations validated by consistency across homologous layers in same-base pairs and absent alignment in cross-family controls.

model: cross-family protein LM for specificity check

novelty.differentiation: Connects transferred prediction bias to interpretable protein representations and variant-effect features rather than generic latent drift.
novelty.caveat: Interpretability angle depends on actually localizing the bias in known biological features.

skipped: Capability / editing and Decision reliability not enumerated this run — domain-generality is first established via evidence + formation-tracing + unit interpretation.

behaviors.discovery_strategy_detail: Reuses Shannon channel-capacity / rate-distortion theory by modeling subliminal learning as a noisy side-channel from teacher outputs to student weights, mapping output-format entropy (argmax tokens → top-k → full logits) to channel-alphabet richness and estimating a per-token transfer bit-rate vs trait complexity.
behaviors.statement: When a same-base student is trained on semantically unrelated teacher outputs, measured trait transfer increases with corpus size and output entropy up to a saturating ceiling, with higher-entropy outputs (logits or top-k distributions) achieving a higher asymptotic transfer level than argmax-only tokens for the same trait.

five_bars.real: the effect appears as a quantitative transfer curve in actual same-base fine-tuning runs
five_bars.nonobvious: it predicts an information-theoretic saturation law and an output-format ordering, not just 'more data helps'
five_bars.specific: monotone rise, saturation, and logits > top-k > argmax under matched token budgets
five_bars.robust: the pattern should hold across small same-base pairs and one larger same-base pair for multiple traits
five_bars.tractable: testable with standard fine-tuning, controlled output formats, and fitted capacity curves

impact.rationale: Capacity ceilings + entropy ordering convert the phenomenon from anecdote to measurable channel science with design and defense implications.

behaviors.notes: Close-pair with B4 (capacity/scaling vs small-sample sufficiency).

mechanisms.hypothesis: A low-dimensional set of distributed behavioral-control features in mid-to-late residual-stream subspaces carries the subliminal trait signal; increasing teacher-output entropy injects more recoverable information into these features, and ablating or projecting out the matched subspace reduces transfer strength.
mechanisms.boring_null: transfer is fully explained by generic training progress or memorization of surface statistics, with no trait-linked subspace whose intervention changes behavior.

data.source: semantically unrelated prompt corpus paired with teacher outputs in three formats (argmax tokens, top-k probabilities, full/logit-derived soft targets); separate trait-elicitation benchmark
data.used_n: 5k, 20k, 80k, 320k tokens per condition
data.note: Matched prompts and token budgets across output formats; evaluate multiple traits and fit transfer-vs-data saturation curves.

novelty.differentiation: Adds an explicit information-theoretic ceiling and ordered comparison across logits/top-k/argmax channels, not just transfer-ratio measurement.
novelty.caveat: The logits-superiority claim is already strongly anticipated by prior and original-paper results.

mechanisms.hypothesis: Subliminal transfer is formed by repeated gradient accumulation onto a small number of shared same-base feature directions already aligned between teacher and student; formation tracing shows these directions absorb trait-relevant updates earlier and more strongly under richer teacher outputs, and causal patching of those directions accounts for most of the behavioral change.
mechanisms.boring_null: weight changes are diffuse and unstructured, with no small set of pre-aligned feature directions explaining the output-format dependence or saturation.

data.source: constructed unrelated-teacher-output corpora + standard activation/weight tracing during student fine-tuning; trait evaluations adapted from prior behavioral-probe setups
data.used_n: checkpoint traces every 50-200 steps across 3-5 seeds per condition
data.note: Compare trajectories under argmax, top-k, and soft-target training and fit how much traced directions explain final transfer.

novelty.differentiation: A corpus-size/entropy/capacity law provides a compact predictive account of when transfer saturates and when coarse outputs fail.
novelty.caveat: Natural quantitative extension rather than a surprising new mechanism.

skipped: Capability / editing, Explaining a model, Decision reliability not enumerated this run — the capacity law is a measurement claim best made via evidence + formation-tracing first.

behaviors.discovery_strategy_detail: Reuses differential-privacy theory: per-example gradient clipping bounds each example's influence and additive noise obscures weak aggregate signals; the subliminal channel is modeled as a low-SNR transmission problem, and a DP-SGD noise threshold is tested as a defense. Distinct from B5 (model compatibility, not a defense).
behaviors.statement: In same-base subliminal-learning fine-tuning, increasing the DP-SGD noise multiplier beyond a model- and task-dependent threshold collapses trait transfer to near-baseline while preserving most nominal task accuracy, with clipping-plus-noise outperforming clipping alone as a defense.

five_bars.real: predicts a concrete defense effect in ordinary fine-tuning pipelines using standard DP machinery
five_bars.nonobvious: claims selective suppression of subliminal transfer rather than uniform degradation of all learning
five_bars.specific: a threshold-like dependence on noise multiplier and a clipping-only vs clipping-plus-noise comparison
five_bars.robust: the defense pattern should replicate across model sizes, traits, and corpora if the channel is low-SNR
five_bars.tractable: DP-SGD libraries and standard eval suites make the experiment straightforward on small models

impact.rationale: A DP-SGD threshold that suppresses subliminal transfer while retaining utility is a practical mitigation lever for shared-base fine-tuning pipelines.

behaviors.notes: Close-pair with B5 (DP defense vs compatibility curve) — both bear on transfer magnitude but via different levers.

mechanisms.hypothesis: DP noise disrupts the accumulation of weak trait-correlated evidence in a small set of decision-relevant residual/MLP features, so audited trait-elicitation decisions become less consistently driven by those features while ordinary task-relevant evidence remains comparatively stable.
mechanisms.boring_null: DP reduces performance only through generic optimization harm, with no disproportionate reduction in trait-linked decision evidence or auditing signatures.

data.source: existing general-task fine-tuning datasets + constructed semantically unrelated teacher-output sets and a held-out trait-elicitation probe battery
data.used_n: 10k-50k training examples per condition; 500-2k probe prompts for auditing
data.note: Sweep clipping norm and noise multiplier; match non-DP and DP models on nominal task accuracy where possible.

novelty.closest: DP-SGD defenses against membership inference / backdoors
novelty.differentiation: Repurposes established privacy-defense machinery to test whether the transferred behavior reliably disappears beyond a noise threshold while task accuracy holds.
novelty.caveat: Defense direction is useful but expected.

mechanisms.hypothesis: The subliminal trait depends on a low-amplitude update subspace in shared same-base representations; DP clipping limits per-example movement into that subspace and noise randomizes its sign across steps, so removing the residual trait-linked subspace after DP has little additional effect compared with a large effect in non-DP training.
mechanisms.boring_null: there is no distinct weak update subspace for the trait, and DP's apparent protection is entirely due to reduced effective learning rate or underfitting.

data.source: controlled unrelated teacher-output corpora with matched nominal-task supervision and post-training activation/intervention analyses
data.used_n: 3-5 seeds for each of 6-10 DP settings plus matched non-DP controls
data.note: Estimate trait-linked update directions from non-DP runs, then test whether DP suppresses occupancy and causal relevance of those directions.

novelty.differentiation: Links clipping and noise to attenuation of the teacher-induced gradient signal hypothesized by subliminal-learning theory.
novelty.caveat: Mechanistic interpretation may remain coarse unless gradient-signal attenuation is directly measured.

skipped: Capability / editing, Complete account, Explaining a model not enumerated this run — a defense claim is first established via decision-audit + mechanistic-evidence of signal attenuation.

behaviors.discovery_strategy_detail: Reuses model-merging / task-arithmetic results by treating the trait as a weight-space vector composable across same-base models, mapping subliminal transfer from data-space to a DATA-FREE weight-space channel and testing whether averaging / task-vector addition transmits the trait with coefficient-scaled magnitude.
behaviors.statement: For same-base models, merging a trait-bearing model into a neutral model via weight averaging or task-vector arithmetic transfers the trait without any generated-data training, and the elicited trait strength increases approximately monotonically with the merge coefficient until ordinary capability degradation appears.

five_bars.real: predicts a directly measurable trait change from standard weight merging alone
five_bars.nonobvious: distinguishes a data-free weight-space channel from the paper's data-mediated channel and predicts coefficient scaling
five_bars.specific: same-base requirement, merge methods, no generated data, monotone transfer with merge coefficient
five_bars.robust: should hold across small and larger same-base pairs and multiple independently induced traits
five_bars.tractable: weight merging and trait probes are cheap, requiring no teacher-generated corpus for the transfer step

impact.rationale: A data-free same-base transfer path is operationally relevant, but much of the behavior-transfer story is already known from model merging / task arithmetic.

behaviors.notes: Strong prior work: Personality Vector (arXiv:2509.19727) modulates personality via model merging; task arithmetic already transmits behaviors. Only the explicit data-free-vs-data-channel contrast + coefficient-scaling law is incremental.

mechanisms.hypothesis: The transferred trait is represented by an approximately linear task vector distributed over attention and MLP blocks, so editing along that vector in a neutral same-base model reproduces the behavioral change and reversing it attenuates the trait; merge coefficient scales occupancy of this vector and thus trait magnitude.
mechanisms.boring_null: merging causes only nonspecific behavioral drift from weight interpolation, with no coherent trait vector that can be edited in or out.

data.source: trait-elicitation and capability-evaluation probes only; transfer is done by weight merging / arithmetic with no generated-data channel
data.used_n: 500-2k probe prompts per trait + standard capability eval subsets
data.note: Create trait-bearing models by prior fine-tuning or known behavioral edits, then merge into neutral same-base models at multiple coefficients.

novelty.closest: Personality Vector: Modulating Personality of LLMs by Model Merging (arXiv:2509.19727)
novelty.differentiation: The incremental piece is contrasting data-free weight-space transfer with subliminal data-channel transfer under a common trait benchmark.
novelty.caveat: Core capability of trait transfer via merging is already established.

mechanisms.hypothesis: A sparse set of layerwise weight-delta components carries most trait-relevant causal effect in the merge; localizing and zeroing those components after merging selectively reduces the transferred trait while leaving the rest of the merged model intact, and their contribution scales with merge coefficient.
mechanisms.boring_null: trait transfer is inseparable from global interpolation effects, so no localized subset of weight deltas has selective causal control over the trait.

data.source: weight-space deltas from trait-bearing vs neutral same-base models + post-merge trait/capability probes
data.used_n: coefficient sweep over 8-12 merge values; 3-5 localization schemes
data.note: Attribution over blocks/modules identifies candidate trait-carrying deltas, then ablate/reinsert them in merged models.

novelty.differentiation: Coefficient-scaling laws could quantify how merge magnitude maps to trait strength and compare with data-mediated transfer scaling.
novelty.caveat: Still low-to-medium novelty given strong prior art.

skipped: Complete account, Explaining a model, Decision reliability not enumerated this run — the data-free channel is first established via editing + mechanistic localization.
